channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 2 dead, thousands of structures destroyed as Palisades Fire continues growing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - Officials confirmed two people were killed and over 5,316 structures have been damaged or destroyed as fire crews struggle to gain containment on the over 19,000-acre Palisades Fire: the most destructive wildfire in Los Angeles history.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about thousands of evacuations and homes burned in Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — At least 10 people were killed and thousands of structures were burned as fierce wildfires raged in the Los Angeles area, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Winter storm plods into the Deep South, prompting states of emergency and school closures
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A powerful winter storm that dumped heavy snow and glazed roads with ice across much of Texas and Oklahoma lumbered eastward into southern U.S. states overnight, prompting governors to declare states of emergency and shuttering schools across the region. Arkansas Gov.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In Los Angeles, people snap up air filters and wear masks against smoke pollution
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — With levels of sooty air pollution far too high across swaths of the Los Angeles area, Dana Benton is sleeping with a mask on, even with her air purifier running.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Letter from Gov. Cox calls for immediate removal of Salt Lake City ICE director
item.description: Gov. Cox signed a letter requesting changes to local immigration standards and the immediate removal of the ICE director in Salt Lake City.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, NewsNation

item.title: PowerSchool hack exposes Indiana students' data
item.description: BROWNSBURG, Ind. -- Multiple Indianapolis area school corporations confirmed an unauthorized user accessed its student information system as part of a national data breach.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Haughn, NewsNation

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: The survey also found that 77 percent of employers said they will adopt the strategy of “reskilling and upskilling.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: TSA reveals 10 most 'unusual' finds at checkpoints in 2024
item.description: A Lego set stuffed with gun parts and a sunglasses case filled with snakes were among some of the top "catches” that officers with the Transportation Security Administration made in 2024, according to the agency.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: All 5 living presidents, political dignitaries attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Political leaders gathered to honor the life of former President Jimmy Carter during his state funeral at the Washington National Cathedral on Thursday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says Newsom should resign as Los Angeles wildfires rage
item.description: The president-elect's criticism of Newsom has sparked a political battle over the fires that have killed at least five people and are far from contained.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jimmy Carter memorial: Suspect accused in Capitol Hill security breach during Trump visit identified
item.description: Capitol police have identified the 35-year-old man accused of trying to light his spray-painted car on fire near where former President Carter was lying in state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LIVE: More evacuations ordered after fast-moving Kenneth fire ignites in Los Angeles area
item.description: Firefighters are racing to contain a new wildfire that broke out close to homes in the area of Calabasas and Hidden Hills, northwest of Los Angeles, on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter lauded for humility and service in Washington before being laid to rest in Georgia
item.description: Former U.S. President Jimmy Carter was honored with the pageantry of a funeral in the nation’s capital before a second service in his tiny Georgia hometown that launched a Depression-era farm boy to the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: LA wildfire evacuee Tiptoe the tortoise escapes Palisades fire
item.description: TikTok influencer Caitlin Doran and her 175 pound tortoise are safe from the Los Angeles Palisades fire.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Indiana man killed girlfriend's 4-month-old puppy: Docs
item.description: An Indiana man faces a felony charge after police say he threw his girlfriend's dog onto the ground in frustration, killing the 4-month-old puppy.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, KRON4

item.title: New LA-area fire prompts more evacuations while over 10,000 structures lost to the 2 biggest blazes
item.description: Officials say the two biggest wildfires ravaging the Los Angeles area this week have burned at least 10,000 homes, buildings and other structures.
item.dc:creator: Manuel Valdes, Hallie Golden And Julie Watson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hollywood Hills fire breaks out as deadly wildfires burn out of control across Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A fast-moving fire broke out in the Hollywood Hills on Wednesday night, threatening one of Los Angeles' most iconic spots as firefighters battled to get under control three other major blazes that killed five people, put 130,000 people under evacuation orders and ravaged communiti
item.dc:creator: MANUEL VALDES, HALLIE GOLDEN and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wildfires burning in the Los Angeles area visible from space
item.description: Satellite images from NOAA and CIRA show the Palisades and Eaton Fires burning in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, ABC 4

item.title: Space satellites track astonishing expansion of deadly California wildfires
item.description: Space satellites tracked the flames and smoke caused by eight California wildfires currently scorching the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US will keep protecting more than 2,000 Rocky Mountain grizzly bears
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — The Biden administration said Wednesday it will continue protecting about 2,000 grizzly bears in the Rocky Mountains despite opposition from Republican-led states. Federal officials also said they would ease some restrictions as conflicts between bears and humans rise.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chemical used for explosives found in New Orleans terrorist's storage unit: FBI
item.description: FBI Houston says bottles of sulfuric acid were found inside a cooler at a Harris County storage facility rented by the Bourbon Street terrorist Shamsud-Din Jabbar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Traffic has gotten way worse in these US cities, report finds
item.description: You're not imagining it. Traffic congestion worsened last year in just about every American city studied by navigation and GPS company TomTom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wildfires latest: 2 people killed and over 1,000 structures destroyed, per LA fire chief
item.description: Fierce wildfires are raging in the Los Angeles area, fueled by powerful Santa Ana winds, sending residents fleeing from burning homes through flames, ferocious winds and towering clouds of smoke.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harris's California neighborhood ordered to evacuate as wildfires blaze through area
item.description: Vice President Harris's neighborhood in Los Angeles was ordered to evacuate Tuesday night as wildfires blazed through the area, her spokesperson said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department says it plans to release only part of special counsel's Trump report for now
item.description: The Justice Department said Wednesday that it will release special counsel Jack Smith’s findings on Donald Trump’s efforts to undo the results of the 2020 presidential election but will keep under wraps for now the rest of the record focused on the president-elect's hoarding of classified documents
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 fraternity members at San Diego State University charged after one was set on fire during a 'skit': DA
item.description: The incident occurred last February, when the fraternity was on probation with the university over issues of hazing and conduct that threatens the health and safety of students.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KRON4

item.title: Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app. Lemon8 resembles an amalgamation of the types of short-form videos found on TikTok and the picture-perfect aesthetic of Instagram and Pinterest.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter lies in state at Capitol Rotunda, Trump among those paying respect
item.description: President Jimmy Carter will lie in state for a second day Wednesday after his remains arrived in Washington a day earlier as part of state funeral rites.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Salt Lake City will be one of the most competitive housing markets in 2025, Zillow report predicts
item.description: The hottest housing markets this year are predicted to be on the East Coast, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's rolls back diversity goals
item.description: Fast food giant McDonald’s has become the latest company to roll back diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) commitments following the Supreme Court’s 2023 decision ending affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, ABC 4

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: President Joe Biden said early Wednesday that he thinks he would have beaten President-elect Donald Trump if he stuck with his reelection bid in an interview with USA Today.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Carter continues to lie in state at Capitol Rotunda ahead of his state funeral
item.description: President Jimmy Carter will continue to lie in state Wednesday after his remains arrived in Washington a day earlier as part of state funeral rites.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California governor says Pacific Palisades wildfire has destroyed many structures as winds kick up
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A wildfire whipped up by extreme winds swept through a Los Angeles hillside dotted with celebrity residences Tuesday, burning homes in Pacific Palisades and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JANIE HAR and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cybertruck driver used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas attack, police say
item.description: Police found a 6-page manifesto on Matthew Livelsberger’s phone and say he used ChatGPT to plan his New Year's Day bombing at the front of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas, the LVMPD said at a news conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, Fox 5

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lawsuit alleges ex-Fox Sports host Skip Bayless harassed hairstylist, offered her $1.5 million for sex
item.description: The complaint claims Fox executives fostered a hostile work environment that allowed senior managers and on-air personalities including Bayless to abuse workers without fear of punishment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
